stories.title: Canada GP Sprint Weekend Preview — First Sprint at Gilles-Villeneuve, May 22-24 (TIER 1)

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
One week until Montreal.

The 2026 Canadian GP (May 22-24) will be the FIRST sprint weekend ever held at Circuit Gilles-Villeneuve.

And the championship leader is on a historic run heading in.

Buckle up. #F1 #CanadaGP

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Circuit Gilles-Villeneuve facts:
— 70 laps, 4.361km
— Low downforce, heavy braking
— The famous Wall of Champions
— First sprint weekend in circuit history

Canada GP is May 22-24. Championship is on the line. #F1 #CanadaGP

article.headline: Canada GP Sprint Weekend Preview: First-Ever Sprint at Gilles-Villeneuve, May 22-24

research.facts:
Canada GP Sprint Preview
**Key Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Event: Formula 1 Lenovo Grand Prix du Canada 2026
- Date: May 22-24, 2026 at Circuit Gilles-Villeneuve, Montreal
- Sprint weekend — first EVER sprint at this circuit
- Schedule: FP1 + Sprint Qualifying (Friday), Sprint Race + Grand Prix Qualifying (Saturday), Race (Sunday)
- Race distance: 70 laps, 4.361km circuit
- This is first Canadian GP in May since the race began here in 1982 (moved from June)
- Circuit type: low-downforce, stop-start, heavy-braking chicanes, hairpin, Wall of Champions
- Temperatures: mid-high teens; possibility of showers
- Championship leader Antonelli arrives with 100 pts, 20-pt lead over Russell
- ADUO available to manufacturers AFTER Canada (5th race of season)

stories.title: Antonelli's Historic Run — First Driver to Win First Three Races from Pole (TIER 1)

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Kimi Antonelli is 19 years old.

He has won his first 3 Grand Prix starts — all from pole position.

No driver in F1 history had ever done that.

He also holds the record as the youngest championship leader ever. #F1 #Mercedes

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
After 4 F1 race weekends in 2026:

Antonelli: 100 pts
Russell: 80 pts
Leclerc: 59 pts
Hamilton: 51 pts
Norris: 51 pts
Verstappen: 26 pts

This championship has one gear and it is labeled "Antonelli." #F1

article.headline: Antonelli Makes F1 History: First Driver to Win First Three Races from Pole

research.facts:
Antonelli Historic Season Start
**Key Facts (HIGH confidence — multiple sources including formula1.com, Yahoo Sports, motorsport.com):**
- Antonelli (Mercedes) is first driver in F1 history to win first three races from pole position
- Wins: Chinese GP, Japanese GP, Miami GP
- Also has 3 poles (matching the 3 wins — 3-from-3 pole-to-win)
- Current standings: 100 pts with 20-pt lead over Russell (80 pts)
- Youngest championship leader in F1 history: 19 years, 7 months, 4 days (at time of taking lead)
- Strongest points-scoring start to an F1 season in 10 years per formula1.com
- Miami GP win: overcame Leclerc and Norris in "wheel-to-wheel exchanges" per F1.com
- At Canada, Antonelli goes for first sprint win and 4th GP win to extend lead

stories.title: Verstappen on Lambiase Departure: "You'd Be Crazy Not to Take It" (TIER 2)

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Verstappen on Lambiase's McLaren move:

"He told me about the offer he received. I told him, 'You'd be crazy not to take it.'"

Four words to sum up how much has changed at Red Bull in 2026. #F1 #Verstappen

article.headline: Verstappen on Lambiase's McLaren Move: "You'd Be Crazy Not to Take It"

research.facts:
Verstappen on Lambiase
**Key Facts (HIGH confidence — GP Fans, F1.com, GP Blog, Sky Sports F1):**
- Lambiase confirmed move to McLaren (joining from 2028, when Red Bull contract expires 2027)
- Verstappen quote: "He told me about the offer he received. I told him, 'You'd be crazy not to take it.'"
- Verstappen also: "We've achieved so much together and now he has a chance to move forward."
- Red Bull TP Laurent Mekies: Lambiase "is going to be a team principal" at McLaren (a McLaren description)
- McLaren TP Andrea Stella: played down reports of "astronomical salaries and mythical pre-contracts"
- Ford (Red Bull engine partner): "F1 commitment not based on one person"
- Red Bull maintains Lambiase will serve out his contract through 2027
- Verstappen exit clause: Hungarian GP July 24-26
- Verstappen current standing: 7th with 26 points (Red Bull struggling with 2026 regs)
- Various reports on Verstappen-McLaren talks (Saward, GrandPrix247)

stories.title: FIA Confirms Mid-Season Power Unit Rule Change — ADUO Arrives After Canada (TIER 2)

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
After the Canadian GP, the FIA unlocks ADUO for struggling power unit manufacturers.

Honda and Audi — both dealing with early-season difficulties — can begin accelerated development.

The second half of 2026 just got more interesting. #F1

article.headline: FIA Unlocks Mid-Season Power Unit Development After Canada GP: What ADUO Means for Honda and Audi

research.facts:
FIA Power Unit Rule Change
**Key Facts (HIGH confidence — Sky Sports F1, Planet F1, formula1.com):**
- FIA confirmed mid-season tweak to 2026 power unit regulations
- ADUO (Accelerated Development and Update Option) available to manufacturers after 5th race (Canada)
- Honda (Aston Martin) and Audi (Stake F1/Sauber) expected to benefit
- FIA also confirmed qualifying format tweak alongside PU rule change
- 2026 power unit: 50/50 electric-ICE split; new MGU-K at 350kW (vs 120kW in 2025); no MGU-H
- Power unit manufacturers: Mercedes, Ferrari, Red Bull-Ford, Honda (Aston Martin), Audi
- 2026 is Audi's first season in F1 (took over Sauber)
- Audi experienced "disastrous F1 Miami GP"

stories.title: Ferrari's Title Fight — Hamilton and Leclerc Need Canada to Narrow the Gap (TIER 2)

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Lewis Hamilton has 6 wins at Circuit Gilles-Villeneuve.

He and Leclerc trail Antonelli by 41 and 20 points respectively.

If Ferrari needs a statement race, there are few better tracks for it than Montreal. #F1 #Ferrari

article.headline: Ferrari's Canada Mission: Hamilton and Leclerc Must Strike Before Europe Takes Over

research.facts:
Ferrari Canada Preview
**Key Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Leclerc: 3rd in drivers' championship, 59 pts
- Hamilton: 4th, 51 pts (joined Ferrari for 2026)
- Combined Ferrari driver points: 110
- Antonelli+Russell: 180 pts
- Hamilton is 6-time winner at Circuit Gilles-Villeneuve (HIGH — well-documented historical fact)
- Circuit Gilles-Villeneuve historically suits cars with strong mechanical grip and effective braking
- Ferrari constructors points vs Mercedes constructors: need to verify
- Per yesterday's pipeline: "Mercedes constructors dominant at 180 pts" (MEDIUM — from prior research)
- Canadian GP low downforce — historically good for cars with efficient ERS and braking
